identity.short_name: Gen-4 capstone: mixed $2^6p^4$ slice count via CRT and square roots mod $2^7$

subdomain(s): counting solutions to congruences modulo prime powers
Chinese remainder theorem and mixed-modulus systems
Let $T=\left(\frac{q}{p}\right)$ and let $s$ be the unique integer $0\le s<p$ with $s\equiv a+Tb\pmod p$.
Let $M=64p^4$ and let $S=\\{(x,y)\\in(\\mathbb{Z}/M\\mathbb{Z})^2:\\ x^2+y^2\\equiv p\\pmod M,\\ x\\equiv sy\\pmod p,\\ x\\equiv sy+1\\pmod{64}\\}$.
Let $N=|S|$ and let $A$ be the unique integer $0\le A<997$ with $A\equiv N\pmod{997}$.
the coupling $s\equiv a+Tb\pmod p$ links reciprocity with the norm representation
the modulus $p^4$ forces a nontrivial Hensel lift depth for the slice parameter $k$
fixed sign T and fixed residue s
identity simplifying both p-adic and 2-adic expansions
reduced congruence in y,k modulo p^3
polynomial F(k) with F(k0)\equiv0 (mod p) and F'(k0) not \equiv0 (mod p)
unique k modulo p^3 for each admissible y
odd U and one known root $z_0$ modulo $128$
exactly 4 roots for $z^2\equiv U\pmod{128}$ and hence $|S_2|=4$
congruence depending only on y (mod p^3)
invertibility modulo prime powers and lifting solutions
compute s via reciprocity and enforce s^2+1=2p from the consecutive sum-of-two-squares representation
split the count via CRT into p-adic and 2-adic components with coupled slice constraints
count the p-adic component by parametrization and simple-root Hensel lifting to modulus p^3
count the 2-adic component by completing the square and counting square roots modulo 128
compute T via Euler criterion pow(q,(p-1)//2,p) and map to ±1 (or sympy.legendre_symbol)
enumerate y mod 64 to verify |S_2|=4 with x\equivsy+1
iterate y1 mod p^3 with p∤y1, solve F(k)=2y1^2+2sy1k+pk^2-1 \equiv 0 (mod p^3) by Hensel lifting, lift y=y1+p^3 t (t=0..p-1), set x\equivsy+pk (mod p^4), verify x^2+y^2\equivp (mod p^4), and count
the p-adic verification loops over p^3≈6.9×10^4 base classes and p lifts each, about 2.8×10^6 checks, feasible without brute-forcing pairs (x,y)
